commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b8c6ca738bf6a5d7d333c164bb7d937076cd3eee)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a five-model OpenCode Zen fallback catalog, live `/zen/v1/models` discovery, shared OpenCode credential lookup, URL normalization, manifest catalog rows, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +435, Tests +239. Total +674 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main lacks the former catalog, and the proposed transport mismatch is directly reproducible by comparing the builder with OpenCode’s current model endpoint table; no credentialed model call was run in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocking defect is a concrete provider-local mapping repair: restore per-family transport/base-URL selection and focused tests, while leaving fallback-scope approval and real proof to maintainers and the contributor.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Restore a plugin-owned OpenCode Zen model catalog for the bundled `opencode` provider.
- Wire Zen catalog runtime hooks: static curated seed, live `/zen/v1/models` discovery, dynamic model resolution, model-catalog augmentation, and base URL normalization.
- Add focused coverage for static Claude seed rows, live-discovered-only rows, shared OpenCode auth handling, runtime discovery metadata, and stale Zen base URL canonicalization.

Fixes #92479

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** `opencode` no longer ships without catalog or dynamic-resolution hooks. A configured Zen model such as `opencode/claude-opus-4-8` can resolve from the bundled curated seed, and live discovery can synthesize additional Zen rows such as `gpt-5.5` from `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models` while falling back to static rows when discovery is unavailable.

**Environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2. Local proof used a dummy OpenCode env key for catalog fallback visibility and mocked live discovery for live-only row synthesis; no real OpenCode credentials or live model requests were used.

**Command run after the patch:**

```bash
git diff --check
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . test extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . exec oxlint extensions/opencode/index.ts extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts extensions/opencode/index.test.ts
OPENCODE_API_KEY=dummy corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . openclaw models list --provider opencode
corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir . plugins:inventory:check
```

**Observed result:**

```text
git diff --check produced no output

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 56.66s

oxlint produced no output

Model                                      Input      Ctx         Local Auth  Tags
opencode/claude-fable-5                    text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-6                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-7                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-opus-4-8                   text+image 195k        no    yes
opencode/claude-sonnet-4-6                 text+image 195k        no    yes

plugins:inventory:check completed successfully
```

## Notes

This follows the default direction proposed in the issue: mirror the `opencode-go` catalog pattern, keep a curated Claude-family static seed, and enable runtime discovery for the Zen endpoint. Full `pnpm build` was attempted locally, but the `tsdown` phase was killed with SIGTERM after a long no-output period without a TypeScript diagnostic, so it is not claimed as passing here.
target.author: mushuiyu886
